title: Finishing the twin - DRM.jl + drmTMB
subtitle: Evidence-led mission control for the DRM.jl <-> drmTMB twin, now running R-first: native drmTMB support/status first, Julia acceleration and parity second. A slice is not done unless engine support, R bridge support, point estimates, CI/status, tests, docs/articles, visuals, issue evidence, and Rose audit agree.

slices.name: Standardize interval statuses across summary, confint, diagnostics, and bridge payloads
Promote Gaussian sd:mu:phylo target only with point, logLik, profile, bootstrap, refit-count, and failure-reason parity
Require no speed headline without point-estimate and inference-status evidence
Add native R/TMB, R-to-Julia, and direct Julia comparison rows for promoted cells
Random slopes: fixed-effect likelihoods, independent slopes, correlated slopes, then structured slopes
Non-Gaussian: coefficient parity before variance/correlation and CI claims
Bivariate rho12: keep residual correlation separate from structured and group-level correlations
Mixed and cross-family models: keep Gaussian plus non-Gaussian correlations experimental until recovery and CI evidence pass
High-q correlations: q4 first, q8 second, higher only after transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status gates
Structural dependencies: animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE provenance/recovery/bridge/docs/visual gates
Benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl as separate routes
Separate cold start, warm start, marshalling, Julia kernel, R reconstruction, profile batches, and bootstrap batches
Borrow HSquared AI-REML lessons only for exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells
Use observed-information Newton, Fisher/natural-gradient, exact AD/implicit gradients, or Takahashi/selected-inverse methods only after derivation
Record estimates, logLik/objective, CI endpoints/status, convergence, versions, threads, BLAS/OpenMP, memory, and dirty flags
Stream long benchmark partial rows without overwriting full artifacts
Keep GPU/backend vocabulary planned until evidence exists
Implement observed-response masks as likelihood/FIML-style handling, not mean imputation
Complete-data all-true mask equals current complete-data logLik exactly
Bivariate partial-pair behaviour documented and tested
Missing-response bridge passes R-to-Julia-to-R for advertised families
Missing-predictor policy is explicit and separate from response masks
Missingness heatmap, diagnostics, article, parity tests, and issue links

phases.name: Visuals and articles built as we go

Pat and Darwin applied-user walkthrough before tutorial promotion
Florence, Fisher, Pat, Grace, and Rose review before done language
Visit GLLVM.jl/gllvmTMB before bridge, mixed-family, missingness, structural, and benchmark promotion
slices.evidence: GLLVM finish plan reviewed and dashboard lessons imported

slices.name: Visit HSquared.jl/hsquared before REML/AI-style, variance-component CI, matrix, and dashboard promotion
Write scout note or issue comment for each imported idea
Borrow patterns, not claims; Rose checks borrowed terminology
Start big simulations only after smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass
ADEMP grids cover Gaussian, non-Gaussian, mixed-family, random slopes, high-q, missingness, structures, and bridge cells
Coverage reports include usable-CI denominators and MCSE
Comparator checks use same-estimand targets and record unavailable comparators honestly

CRAN submission

finish_board.blocker: #585, #588, #591, and the 500-replicate interval artifact are banked. Scope remains fixed-effect native TMB only; Julia bridge, random effects, structured effects, profile/bootstrap intervals, headline coverage, and release readiness stay unsupported or planned.

Contract and merged implementation still agree: fixed-effect mu only. The 500-replicate interval artifact audits Wald intervals for the supported fixed-effect cells, not sigma, rho12, random/structured, bivariate, non-logit, proportion-plus-weights, or Julia bridge routes.

#587 is merged. The registry, generated capability comparison, drift guard, and dashboard heatmap are banked; bridge promotion still waits for per-cell parity evidence and an explicit decision on R-gated Julia-covered cells.

The widget schema, validator, served dashboard, canonical team roster, issue-led rows, and evidence-link checks are maintained in docs/dev-log/dashboard; each new finish slice must keep these rows current.
Keep this as the lead novelty; do not let speed or scale-phylo become the headline before evidence matures.
Owner decides whether to open a DRM.jl issue or maintainer note. Codex does not modify DRM.jl code, and drmTMB makes no binomial Julia-bridge claim.

finish_board.family: bridge capability ledger and cbind-binomial transport lesson
finish_board.blocker: Borrow the capability-ledger pattern, not gllvmTMB's broader multivariate model scope.
Borrow status discipline; do not transfer AI-REML language to Laplace or non-Gaussian cells.

finish_board.family: glm parity plus MCSE-backed fixed-effect Wald intervals
finish_board.blocker: The 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald interval artifact is banked: 3,000/3,000 fits ok, pdHess rate 1.000, zero failures, coverage 0.946-0.964 with max MCSE 0.01010782. Broader headline coverage, profile/bootstrap intervals, random/structured/bivariate routes, and Julia bridge support remain planned or unsupported.

The ADEMP/Williams guard-sensitivity design, first fixed-effect Gaussian log(sigma) clamp pilot, first Student-t finite-variance diagnostic pilot, skew-normal tail-floor source and fit-stress diagnostics, beta/zero-one beta support-floor diagnostic, residual rho12 open-interval diagnostic, q2 mu/sigma covariance boundary diagnostic, and ordinary q2 correlation-grid diagnostic are banked. The log(sigma) pilot has 300/300 fits ok, negligible default-vs-off differences when inactive (max logLik diff 2.046363e-11), a wide band that matches the unclamped reference, and material default-band changes in out-of-band huge/tiny scale fits. The Student-t pilot has 50 fixed-effect shape fits and surfaces near-boundary student_nu warnings/errors without making coverage, power, interval-calibration, release, or CRAN claims. The skew-normal source diagnostic shows ordinary alpha*z values are unchanged to numerical tolerance, while floor-dominated extreme tails are capped at log(1e-300). The fit-stress diagnostic ran 9 fits with generating-scale floor exposure but no fitted-scale floor-dominated observations; it also retained one ordinary-reference non-converged, non-positive-Hessian fit with a large slant diagnostic. The support-floor diagnostic shows source-level beta shape floors activating in high-scale source cells, 6/6 small fitted cells converging with pdHess = TRUE, 0 reported shape-floor activations among the 4 fitted response-route cells that expose shape vectors, missing-predictor fitted shape counts recorded as NA, retained check_drm warning rows, and 6/6 boundary validation cells failing visibly. The rho12 diagnostic shows 4/4 fixed residual-correlation stress fits converged with pdHess = TRUE while still surfacing 2/4 default starting-value clamps, 2/4 fixed-gradient warnings, and one default rho12_boundary warning at fitted rho12 = 0.9813. The q2 mu/sigma diagnostic shows 4/4 Gaussian mu/sigma covariance stress fits converged with pdHess = TRUE while the true rho = 0.98 cell fit at rho = 0.999999 and now surfaces a mu_sigma_random_effect_covariance warning. The ordinary q2 correlation-grid diagnostic shows 12/12 fitted ordinary q2 covariance cells converged with pdHess = TRUE, 8/12 default-gradient-ok cells, and four route-specific fitted-boundary warnings across univariate mu/sigma, bivariate mu1/mu2, and bivariate sigma1/sigma2 routes. Broader structured correlation guards, interval coverage, random/structured/bivariate scale promotion, and release claims remain planned.

finish_board.family: release gate separated from local package health
finish_board.blocker: The fixed-effect binomial GLM parity artifact, fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, log(sigma) guard pilot, and fixed-effect skew-normal diagnostic pilot are banked, but release gates remain deferred until broader evidence, pkgdown, comparator, widget, and issue ledgers agree.

matrix.next: Keep status.json, sweep.json, check-log, and after-task notes synchronized with issues.
Wire README, ROADMAP, NEWS, pkgdown, Documenter, and dashboard claims to this matrix.
#587 is merged. Review remaining R-gated Julia-covered cells before any bridge promotion; do not imply user-selectable Julia-side controls.
Promote only after native R, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct Julia point estimate plus CI/status parity are recorded in one row.
Fixed-effect likelihoods first, independent slopes second, correlated slopes third, structured slopes last.

Coefficient parity first; variance, correlation, and CI claims require their own recovery rows. Fixed-effect skew-normal now has a six-cell diagnostic pilot, but formal calibration and comparator evidence remain planned.

#585, #588, #589, #591, and the 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald interval artifact are banked. Fixed-effect stats::binomial(logit) fits have stats::glm() parity, clearer docs, and MCSE-backed Wald interval evidence for six audited cells; random effects, structured effects, bivariate/mixed responses, profile/bootstrap intervals, headline coverage, and the Julia bridge remain planned or unsupported.
Keep residual rho12 separate from group, phylogenetic, spatial, kernel, and cross-family correlations.
Use DRM.jl#280-style recovery and bridge labels before user-facing promotion.
q4 first, q8 second; higher q requires transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status evidence.
Animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE rows each need provenance, PSD/name-alignment tests, recovery, bridge parity, docs, and visuals.
No speed headline without point-estimate and CI/status evidence; benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl separately.
Borrow hsquared only as a design analogue for exact Gaussian MME cells; use the actual method name for Laplace/non-Gaussian cells.
Use likelihood/FIML-style masks; complete-data all-true masks must match current complete-data log-likelihood exactly.
Every major capability needs a real visual: heatmap, profile curve, parity plot, missingness heatmap, structural visual, and runtime-plus-CI plot where relevant.

Binomial fixed-effect glm parity, the 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, the first log(sigma) clamp sensitivity pilot, the first fixed-effect skew-normal diagnostic pilot, and the q8 staged diagnostic artifact are banked; broader guard classes, q8 coverage/power, same-response hardening, visual artifacts, and release-readiness evidence remain partial or planned.

The first binomial comparator, fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, log(sigma) guard pilot, and skew-normal diagnostic pilot are banked, but release notes still must separate local package health, public CI/pkgdown state, speed evidence, unsupported cells, and remaining validation debt. No CRAN submission without user decision.

active_work.text: The one active finish-board row remains drmTMB#59 numerical-guard sensitivity. This slice banks ordinary q2 fitted-boundary visibility across univariate mu/sigma, bivariate mu1/mu2, and bivariate sigma1/sigma2 covariance routes beside the residual rho12, beta/zero-one beta support-floor, skew-normal source/fit-stress, log(sigma), and Student-t guard artifacts; metrics remain unchanged.

The ordinary q2 correlation-grid artifact is diagnostic evidence only: 12/12 fitted ordinary q2 covariance cells converged with pdHess = TRUE, but 8/12 were default-gradient-ok and the largest absolute fitted-minus-true correlation difference was 0.4083517. It records fitted-boundary visibility, not interval, coverage, power, or recovery accuracy.

Post-#624 main gates are green at 6bd81b0d: R-CMD-check run 27790909354 passed on macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows; pkgdown/Pages run 27792296045 passed with pkgdown in 22m21s and deploy in 8s. Pages returned HTTP 200 with last-modified Thu, 18 Jun 2026 22:29:51 GMT. Mission-control status remains 25/68 banked or verified, 1 active, 0 blocked, and 1 deferred.

The ordinary q2 correlation-grid diagnostic reports 4 source rows and 12 fitted cells across univariate mu/sigma, bivariate mu1/mu2, and bivariate sigma1/sigma2 routes: 12/12 converged with pdHess = TRUE, 8/12 fixed-gradient checks ok, 8/12 check_drm warning-or-error rows, and four route-specific covariance warnings. Structured correlation guards, interval coverage, q4/q8 covariance intervals, random effects in rho12, and release claims remain future evidence.

activity.text: Banked an ordinary q2 random-effect correlation-grid diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: univariate mu/sigma, bivariate mu1/mu2, and bivariate sigma1/sigma2 covariance routes were crossed with true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98, with fitted-boundary status, gradients, Hessian status, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Refreshed mission-control current-state evidence after PR #624 merged: the dashboard now records post-#624 R-CMD-check and pkgdown/Pages evidence for 6bd81b0d while keeping drmTMB#59 active and metrics unchanged.

Banked a q2 mu/sigma random-effect covariance boundary diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98 under matching Gaussian location-scale random intercepts, with boundary distance, gradients, Hessian status, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Banked a residual rho12 open-interval diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98 under fixed rho12 ~ 1, with start clamps, fixed-gradient warnings, boundary distance, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Banked a beta/zero-one beta support-floor diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: source floors activate in high-scale guard cells, fitted response-route shape reports stayed above the floor, missing-predictor fitted shape counts remain unexposed, and boundary validation failures remained visible.

Banked a skew-normal tail-floor fit-stress diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: 9 fixed-effect fits, generating-scale floor exposure in injected cells, zero fitted floor-dominated observations, and one retained ordinary-reference non-converged row with a large slant diagnostic.

Banked a skew-normal tail-floor source diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: ordinary alpha*z values changed by at most 4.434133e-17 log units, while extreme floor-dominated tails are capped at log(1e-300).

Refreshed mission-control current-state evidence after PR #617 merged: the dashboard now records post-#617 R-CMD-check and pkgdown evidence for c57160a while keeping drmTMB#59 active and metrics unchanged.

Synchronized the numerical-guard audit ledger so the banked Student-t finite-variance diagnostic pilot is recorded beside the first log(sigma) clamp pilot, with coverage, power, interval-calibration, release, and CRAN boundaries left planned.
Added robust Student-t vignette guidance showing how to extract the `check_drm()` `student_nu` row and keep its status beside AIC, coefficient, and simulation-summary tables.

Banked a small Student-t finite-variance-boundary diagnostic pilot: 50 fixed-effect Student-t shape fits across low-nu and ordinary-nu cells, with explicit `student_nu` warning/error/note/ok rates for the numerical-guard sensitivity ledger.
Added Student-t shape simulation diagnostic columns for `check_drm()` `student_nu` status, value, and message, so future finite-variance-boundary guard pilots can report warning rates explicitly.

Refreshed mission-control current-state text after PR #612 merged: the dashboard now names drmTMB#59 numerical-guard sensitivity as the one active finish-board row and records post-#612 main R-CMD-check plus pkgdown evidence at 6386eb8.

Closed Phase 1 Rose signoff after correcting stale missing-data release wording in known limitations, adding that ledger to the public-claim validator, and refreshing stale dashboard active-work text.

This adds execution plumbing and artifacts only; it does not promote power, coverage, speed, release, model, formula, or likelihood claims.

Banked the first fixed-effect skew-normal diagnostic pilot: six cells, 25 replicates per cell, 150/150 ok fits, pdHess rate 1.000, and slant-term Wald coverage kept as pilot evidence.

blockers.item: R-first pivot: do not make the Julia route the only usable path for Ayumi-style q4 workflows.
Do not frame Ayumi's q4 route as a native-TMB restricted-likelihood gap; describe the supported estimator and evidence cells explicitly.
Big ADEMP/power grids wait until smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass.

A numerical guard can prevent overflow or singular covariance evaluations, but it must not upgrade convergence or inference claims unless sensitivity simulations show negligible impact in the intended operating range. The ADEMP design is banked; broader guard-class simulations are still planned.

evidence.text: The numerical-guard audit now has an ADEMP/Williams simulation contract with guard-class DGP lanes, estimands, methods, performance measures, MCSE tiers, a Williams 11-item self-audit, and a constant-classification rule. This banks the design for the next big simulations, not new guard-performance evidence.

The fixed-effect skew-normal pilot has 150/150 ok fits, convergence and pdHess rates of 1.000 in all six cells, zero warnings, maximum slant-term absolute bias 0.3067476, and 70% slant Wald coverage from 0.64 to 0.96 with MCSE up to 0.096, so it supports further formal grid work but not calibrated interval promotion.

The log(sigma) clamp sensitivity pilot has 300/300 ok fits: default-vs-off max logLik difference is 2.046363e-11 when inactive, the wide band matches the unclamped reference across all cells, and the default band changes out-of-band scale fits by up to 526.8952 logLik units and 32.38647 on the sigma intercept.

The binomial GLM parity artifact has 6/6 converged fits, 6/6 pdHess TRUE fits, zero failures, maximum coefficient difference 1.894251e-11, maximum SE difference 6.393821e-08, maximum logLik difference 2.728484e-12, and maximum AIC/BIC difference 5.456968e-12.
Mission-control finish board now distinguishes merged binomial implementation/evidence/docs from planned binomial interval calibration and planned numerical-guard sensitivity simulations.
The numerical-guard audit note lists correlation open-interval guards, finite-variance Student-t shape restriction, support floors, and the Gaussian log(sigma) clamp as separate simulation targets.
Julia capability comparison renders from docs/dev-log/dashboard/julia-capabilities.tsv and mirrors to inst/extdata/julia-capabilities.tsv for R CMD check visibility.
Bridge gate registry renders from docs/dev-log/dashboard/julia-gates.tsv; tools/write-julia-gate-registry.R regenerates it from drm_julia_intentional_gates().
drmTMB#577 now owns the finish-board widget; drmTMB#569, #544, and #491 carry the binomial, bridge-gate, and queue-order consensus comments.
Source lives under docs/dev-log/dashboard, validates with tools/validate-mission-control.py, and serves from /tmp/drm-dashboard.
drmTMB#555 is the active Ayumi R-first workflow tracker; drmTMB#544 is closed after the first bridge-gate registry/capability guard, with follow-on bridge promotion handled by new parity issues.
Merged #565 makes Ayumi q4 Julia target inventory report fitted phylocov SDs, not placeholder 0.5 values; the admitted all-q4 bootstrap smoke returned 2/2 refits at 30 tips.

Merged DRM.jl main 9bdea65 verified through the drmTMB Ayumi harness: Julia ML and REML point fits return at 30 tips; ML bootstrap returns four q4 SD rows; REML bootstrap failure is now explicit and tracked.
Native TMB ML bootstrap smokes are negative evidence for fallback wording: 30-tip and 100-tip Ayumi runs returned bootstrap_unavailable after both bootstrap refits failed.
Native TMB careful optimizer settings rescue the 30-tip bootstrap smoke, but neither careful nor robust rescues the 100-tip smoke; the next claim needs per-refit diagnostics and retry evidence.
Current Ayumi reply boundary: Julia B=2 all-q4 bootstrap is plumbing evidence only; native bootstrap/profile intervals are not yet a usable q4 uncertainty fallback; no AI-REML or 10k speed claim.
HSquared scout confirms AI-REML wording belongs only to exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells; Ayumi's bivariate q4 sigma-phylo route still needs point/profile/bootstrap/speed evidence, not an AI-REML label.
Native R/TMB status rows now precede Julia speed work: #552/#553 banked q4 profile status, #557 banked REML rejection gates, and #559 banked the harness.
Rows separate engine, bridge, point estimate, Wald, profile, bootstrap, docs, visual, simulation, and release status.
Unsupported, planned, experimental, partial, covered, verified, banked, active, queued, blocked, and deferred are kept distinct.
